---
title: "Harborlight Heat & Shade Atlas"
description: "A synthetic project testing how a fictional team could prioritize shade-planning evidence."
project-id: DA-EO-001
iteration: "Pilot iteration (synthetic)"
owner: "Nia Calder (fictional)"
steward: "Omar Reyes (fictional)"
phase: "Evidence review and preservation"
synthetic: true
---

::: {.callout-warning}
## Synthetic training record

Harborlight, every named person and location, all dates, measurements, findings, feedback, and decisions on these pages are fabricated. They do not describe a real participant, partner, place, or project.
:::

## Purpose and intended users

The Harborlight Heat & Shade Atlas demonstrates how a project team might organize synthetic observations about afternoon heat and pedestrian shade into reviewable deliverables. Its fictional intended users are a community planning workshop, a program leadership group, and a future Developers Academy cohort practicing evidence handoff.

The project tests workflow clarity, not environmental findings. No production dataset or live application exists.

## Current state

- **Stable project identifier:** `DA-EO-001`
- **Iteration:** Pilot iteration (synthetic), not a cohort-year identity
- **Phase:** Evidence review and preservation
- **Owner:** Nia Calder (fictional)
- **Repository steward:** Omar Reyes (fictional)
- **Next project action:** translate the fictional leadership decision for D01 into its illustrative Issue record, revise D02, and preserve any newly accepted version through review

GitHub Issues would remain authoritative for each live deliverable. This static reference shows a metadata snapshot only. No synthetic GitHub Issues were created, and the deliverable identifiers below are not links to live work.

## Deliverables

| ID | Deliverable | Text status | Next action |
|---|---|---|---|
| `DA-EO-001-D01` | [Priority Locations Decision Brief](deliverables/priority-locations-decision-brief.qmd) | `leadership-review` | Obtain one fictional option decision through the documented non-GitHub path, then translate it manually. |
| `DA-EO-001-D02` | [Synthetic Heat-Pattern Methods Note](deliverables/synthetic-methods-note.qmd) | `changes-requested` | Clarify units and missing-value handling, then return to `internal-review`. |
| `DA-EO-001-D03` | [Project Scope and Data-Safety Brief](deliverables/scope-and-data-safety-brief.qmd) | `accepted` | Preserve the accepted record; curate it into Developedia only if an approved Issue later identifies lasting value. |

The [project catalog](../index.qmd) also renders these deliverables as a Quarto listing.

## Iteration history

| Iteration | Fictional outcome |
|---|---|
| Pilot setup | Assigned stable identity, synthetic owners, and data-safety boundaries. |
| Pilot evidence pass | Drafted five fabricated location observations and a methods note. |
| Pilot review pass | Sent D01 to fictional leadership review, returned D02 for changes, and recorded D03 as accepted and preserved. |

## Decisions and rationale

1. **Use only synthetic evidence.** The pilot validates platform workflow without exposing real locations, people, partner needs, or restricted data.
2. **Keep operational decisions in Issues.** Working-document comments may inform a decision, but a steward must translate actionable feedback manually.
3. **Preserve the safety brief in this repository.** The Markdown source is suitable for Git review and supplies a durable example.
4. **Curate selectively.** Acceptance and preservation do not require Developedia publication.

These rationales are stored locally so they remain understandable even if a future external working-document link becomes unavailable.

## Working and durable locations

- **Working documents:** clearly labeled nonfunctional placeholders only; no private Google Workspace link is published.
- **Accepted artifact:** [D03 at its repository-controlled source path](deliverables/scope-and-data-safety-brief.qmd).
- **Application:** none; this is a static reference project.
- **Developedia entry:** this Quarto project summary is a pilot knowledge entry, not proof of leadership acceptance.

## Methods and limitations

The demonstration uses a fabricated five-row table and a synthetic methods narrative. It has not been calibrated, field-tested, peer-reviewed, or used to guide a real decision. The small sample, invented values, simplified scoring, and intentionally incomplete uncertainty treatment make it unsuitable for analysis outside the training scenario.

## Continuation

Read the [cohort handoff](handoff.qmd) before changing the project. It records current state, ownership, blockers, next action, rationale, artifact locations, unresolved work, and maintenance expectations for the next fictional iteration.
